label: Friend Show Screen Reaction
best_use: High-energy social proof format where the subject reacts and pulls someone else into the moment.
ugc_principle: It feels native because people instinctively show surprising things to friends; the social motion makes the video spreadable.

camera_logic.camera_role: front-facing selfie phone held in hand
camera_logic.start_state: subject is already holding the phone and reacting
camera_logic.pickup_behavior: not pickup-focused; the key is the turn or reach toward a friend
camera_logic.framing: mid close-up, wider than pure face reaction so the gesture reads
camera_logic.face_position: subject starts on one side of frame, then reframes as they turn to call someone
camera_logic.movement_style: quick handheld movement, slight swing, energetic reframing
camera_logic.reframe_rule: the frame can widen or swing because the arm extends toward the friend

prompt_skeleton: The subject is holding the phone and reads something surprising.
The frame swings or reframes as they call a friend or try to show the screen.
The reaction feels unplanned and social, not solo-testimonial polished.
Keep the face and the hand movement both visible enough to sell the urgency.
